title: sarcocephalus cordatus
intro: Sarcocephalus cordatus, also known as African teak, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Sarcocephalus cordatus include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Sarcocephalus cordatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
